_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

The admin bypass URL parameter is `admin=true`. For example:

```
https://your-site.com/?admin=true
```

Here's how it works:

```204:205:src/App.jsx
function useAdmin() {
  return new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get("admin") === "true";
```

When `admin=true` is present:

- **Gate bypass** (line 522): The time-gate check is skipped — `const gateOpen = admin || now >= GATE_OPEN`
- **All stops visible** (line 524): All stops are shown regardless of progress/time restrictions

Okay, we need to update our schedule, we're now leaving at 10am. So all the things that we have to be adjusted / removed. Please do NOT ADD ANYTHING NOT ON THIS LIST. PLEASE REMOVE THINGS THAT ARE NOT ON THIS LIST: Here's your updated Dallas Day Trip agenda:
10:00 AM — Uber from Hyatt Regency Frisco → Downtown Dallas (~40 min ride)
10:40 AM — Bounce bag drop at West End (4 bags: 2 suitcases + 2 backpacks, $37.84 pre-paid)
10:50 AM — Sixth Floor Museum (timed entry tickets booked for 10:00-10:30, arriving a bit late — should be fine. Audio guide, ~75 minutes)
12:00 PM — Walk Dealey Plaza, Grassy Knoll & JFK Memorial (find the X marks on Elm Street, stand on the Grassy Knoll — hits harder after the museum exhibit, ~20-30 min)
12:30 PM — Lunch at Meso Maya Downtown, 1611 McKinney Ave (Resy reservation, 2 guests, Dining Room. Mole, margaritas, patio if weather's nice, ~75 min)
2:00 PM — Dallas Arboretum & Botanical Garden / Dallas Blooms (buy tickets at the gate, $17-22/person, ~90 min wandering)
3:30 PM — Uber back to West End, pick up bags from Bounce → Uber to DFW (~25 min to airport)
4:45 PM — AMEX Centurion Lounge, Terminal D (Platinum card gets you in. Craft cocktails, chef-curated bites, ~90 min to decompress)
7:05 PM — Skylink back to Terminal E, board AS 317 at Gate E11 → Seattle

_**User**_

If she's on the page and the card is exposed by going through all the hints, will the next card automatically load at the right time below the previous card? Will the viewport shift down? Does she have to reload? Could we maybe put a counter for the next card reveal too?

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Here's the situation:

**Will the next card auto-appear?** Yes, but only checked every 60 seconds (line 483: `setInterval(..., 60000)` once `started` is true). So there could be up to a ~60s delay before a new card pops in.

**Will the viewport shift down?** No — the new card just appears at the bottom of the list. She'd have to scroll down to see it. There's no auto-scroll.

**Does she have to reload?** No, the timer re-evaluates `visibleStops` each tick. But with the 60s interval, it's a bit sluggish.

Let me improve all three issues: tick faster on the timeline, auto-scroll to a newly appeared card, and add a countdown to the next card reveal below the "More stops will appear..." message.

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Pushed and deploying. Here's what changed:

1. **Auto-appear: Yes** — cards now check every 1 second (was 60s), so a new card will pop in almost instantly when it's time.

2. **Auto-scroll: Now yes** — when a new card appears, the page smoothly scrolls to center it in the viewport. She won't miss it.

3. **No reload needed** — the timer runs continuously in the background.

4. **Next card countdown** — below the "More stops will appear as the day unfolds..." message, there's now a live countdown timer (same gold Courier style as the start page) showing exactly when the next card will be revealed.

---

---

_**User**_

What if we do iOS Chrome?

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Unfortunately, that doesn't help. On iOS, **all browsers** (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, etc.) are required by Apple to use the WebKit engine under the hood. So iOS Chrome has the same limitations as iOS Safari — no Notification API unless the site is installed as a PWA to the Home Screen.

The options for getting her attention on an iPhone are:

1. **PWA + Notification API** — She'd need to "Add to Home Screen" from Safari (not Chrome — only Safari supports PWA install on iOS). After that, the site can send native-looking push banners even when the screen is off. This is the most reliable but requires that one-time setup step.

2. **Sound chime** — Works in any browser, no setup needed. Plays an audio cue when a new card is about to drop. Caveat: won't be heard if the phone is on silent/Do Not Disturb, and iOS requires at least one user tap before audio can play (which she'll already have done by tapping "Begin").

3. **Both** — Set up as a PWA with notifications, and also play a sound as a fallback.

The real question is whether the "Add to Home Screen" step is worth it for a one-day experience, or if a sound chime is good enough since she'll likely have the phone in hand during the trip anyway.

---

_**User**_

Let's not bother with the notifications. What would we need to setup a text message? We'd need somtehing to trigger them. I haave an API that we can use to send them but how would we build sometihng like this on netlify?

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Good thinking — SMS is the most reliable way to reach her on any phone, regardless of browser or silent mode. Here's how it would work on Netlify:

**Architecture:**

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    subgraph netlify [Netlify]
        ScheduledFn["Scheduled Function\n(cron trigger)"]
    end
    ScheduledFn -->|"Check: is it time\nfor next stop?"| SMS_API["Your SMS API"]
    SMS_API -->|"Text message"| Phone["Stefanie's Phone"]
```

**How Netlify handles this:**

Netlify has **Scheduled Functions** — serverless functions that run on a cron schedule. You'd create a single function that:

1. Runs every minute (or every 5 minutes) via cron
2. Checks the current time (CDT) against your stop schedule
3. If a stop is coming up (e.g., 5 minutes before reveal time), calls your SMS API to send a teaser text
4. Tracks what's already been sent (so she doesn't get duplicates) — either via a simple check against the schedule times, or a lightweight KV store

**What you'd need:**

- A **Netlify Scheduled Function** (`netlify/functions/notify.mjs` with a cron schedule)
- Your **SMS API credentials** stored as Netlify environment variables
- The **stop schedule** hardcoded in the function (same times as the app)
- Stefanie's **phone number** (also as an env var)

**No database needed** — since the schedule is fixed and the event is one day, the function just compares "current time in CDT" against the known stop times. If `now` is within the notification window for a stop and that stop's time hasn't passed yet, fire the SMS.

from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
date = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 10, 40),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 10, 50),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 12, 0),
    ('Meso Maya', 12, 30),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 0),
    ('Bag Pickup', 15, 30),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = date.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"
</summary>

```bash
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
date = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 10, 40),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 10, 50),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 12, 0),
    ('Meso Maya', 12, 30),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 0),
    ('Bag Pickup', 15, 30),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = date.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"

```

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Test sent to your phone now with the link included. Check it out and let me know how it looks.

One important thing: **you'll want to cancel the 8 old scheduled messages** (the ones without the link) in your GHL dashboard. Look for conversation ID `dgWoRpkEnPkKkQN1jVDW` and delete the scheduled messages that don't have the URL. Otherwise she'd get duplicate texts at each stop — one without the link and one with.

---

_**User**_

Will that link take her straight to the new card or will it open at the top?

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

It'll open at the top every time — the start/landing page with the cowboy hat and "Tap to Begin". Then she taps to begin, and she's at the top of the timeline. She'd have to scroll down to the new card.

There's no state persistence either, so every time she opens the link, she starts fresh (all cards back to "sealed" state).

We could improve this two ways:

1. **Save progress in `localStorage`** — so when she reopens the link, she's already past the start screen and previously revealed cards stay revealed.

2. **Auto-scroll to the newest card on load** — so when the page opens, it jumps to the latest unrevealed card.

Want me to add both? That would make the text message experience much smoother — tap the link, and she's right at the new riddle with all her previous reveals intact.

---

_**User**_

Sure add both. 

---

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Pushed and deploying. Here's what the SMS experience looks like now:

1. She gets a teaser text with the link
2. She taps https://stefanies-day-in-dallas.netlify.app/
3. **First visit**: She sees the landing page, taps "Begin", and enters the timeline. That `started` state is saved.
4. **Every subsequent visit**: She skips straight to the timeline, and the page auto-scrolls to the newest card. All previously revealed cards stay revealed with their answers showing.

Her progress (which cards she's opened, which hints she's seen) is all stored in `localStorage` and survives closing the browser, reopening from a new text, etc.

Wait — let me think about this carefully. The SMS times are 60 seconds *before* the card reveals. So if SMS #1 is at 9:55 AM, the card should reveal at 9:56 AM. If SMS #2 is at 10:05 AM, the card should reveal at 10:06 AM.

Currently, card reveal = stop time minus 1 hour. So:
- Bounce (10:40 AM) reveals at 9:40 AM — needs to reveal at 9:56 AM instead
- Museum (10:50 AM) reveals at 9:50 AM — needs to reveal at 10:06 AM

The reveal logic uses `stop time - 1 hour`, so I'd need to change the stop times to make the math work, or change the reveal logic for those cards. The cleanest approach: add an optional `revealTime` field to those stops that overrides the default calculation.

```bash
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
d = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)
for label, h, m in [('SMS1', 9, 55), ('SMS2', 10, 5)]:
    t = d.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    print(f'{label}: {t.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")} CDT = utc_ts {int(t.timestamp())}')
"

```

SMS2: 10:05 AM CDT = utc_ts 1773414300

```bash
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
now = datetime.now(CDT)
print(f'Current time CDT: {now.strftime(\"%A %B %d, %Y %I:%M %p\")}')
print(f'UTC timestamp now: {int(now.timestamp())}')

# Check what timestamp we scheduled for SMS1
print(f'SMS1 scheduled ts: 1773413700')
from datetime import datetime
sms1 = datetime.fromtimestamp(1773413700, tz=CDT)
print(f'SMS1 fires at: {sms1.strftime(\"%A %B %d, %Y %I:%M %p CDT\")}')
"

```

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

Now calculate timestamps for the new SMS schedule. Card reveals at stop time minus 1 hour, SMS fires 60 seconds before reveal:

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
d = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 11, 10),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 11, 20),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 12, 35),
    ('Meso Maya', 13, 0),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 30),
    ('Bag Pickup', 16, 0),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = d.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"
</summary>

```bash
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
d = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 11, 10),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 11, 20),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 12, 35),
    ('Meso Maya', 13, 0),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 30),
    ('Bag Pickup', 16, 0),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = d.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"

```

```
Bounce                    stop=11:10 AM  reveal=10:10 AM  sms=10:09 AM  utc_ts=1773414540
Sixth Floor Museum        stop=11:20 AM  reveal=10:20 AM  sms=10:19 AM  utc_ts=1773415140
Dealey Plaza              stop=12:35 PM  reveal=11:35 AM  sms=11:34 AM  utc_ts=1773419640
Meso Maya                 stop=01:00 PM  reveal=12:00 PM  sms=11:59 AM  utc_ts=1773421140
Arboretum                 stop=02:30 PM  reveal=01:30 PM  sms=01:29 PM  utc_ts=1773426540
Bag Pickup                stop=04:00 PM  reveal=03:00 PM  sms=02:59 PM  utc_ts=1773431940
Centurion Lounge          stop=04:45 PM  reveal=03:45 PM  sms=03:44 PM  utc_ts=1773434640
Board Flight              stop=07:05 PM  reveal=06:05 PM  sms=06:04 PM  utc_ts=1773443040

```
</details>
</tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
d = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 11, 30),
    ('Meso Maya', 11, 45),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 13, 0),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 14, 15),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 45),
    ('Bag Pickup', 16, 0),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = d.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"
</summary>

```bash
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
CDT = timezone(timedelta(hours=-5))
d = datetime(2026, 3, 13, tzinfo=CDT)

stops = [
    ('Bounce', 11, 30),
    ('Meso Maya', 11, 45),
    ('Sixth Floor Museum', 13, 0),
    ('Dealey Plaza', 14, 15),
    ('Arboretum', 14, 45),
    ('Bag Pickup', 16, 0),
    ('Centurion Lounge', 16, 45),
    ('Board Flight', 19, 5),
]

for name, h, m in stops:
    stop_time = d.replace(hour=h, minute=m, second=0)
    reveal_time = stop_time - timedelta(hours=1)
    sms_time = reveal_time - timedelta(seconds=60)
    ts = int(sms_time.timestamp())
    print(f'{name:25s} stop={stop_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  reveal={reveal_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  sms={sms_time.strftime(\"%I:%M %p\")}  utc_ts={ts}')
"

```

```
Bounce                    stop=11:30 AM  reveal=10:30 AM  sms=10:29 AM  utc_ts=1773415740
Meso Maya                 stop=11:45 AM  reveal=10:45 AM  sms=10:44 AM  utc_ts=1773416640
Sixth Floor Museum        stop=01:00 PM  reveal=12:00 PM  sms=11:59 AM  utc_ts=1773421140
Dealey Plaza              stop=02:15 PM  reveal=01:15 PM  sms=01:14 PM  utc_ts=1773425640
Arboretum                 stop=02:45 PM  reveal=01:45 PM  sms=01:44 PM  utc_ts=1773427440
Bag Pickup                stop=04:00 PM  reveal=03:00 PM  sms=02:59 PM  utc_ts=1773431940
Centurion Lounge          stop=04:45 PM  reveal=03:45 PM  sms=03:44 PM  utc_ts=1773434640
Board Flight              stop=07:05 PM  reveal=06:05 PM  sms=06:04 PM  utc_ts=1773443040

```
</details>
</tool-use>

| # | SMS Sent | Card Reveals | Stop |

| 3 | **11:59 AM** | 12:00 PM | Sixth Floor Museum |
| 4 | **1:14 PM** | 1:15 PM | Dealey Plaza |
| 5 | **1:44 PM** | 1:45 PM | Arboretum |

| 7 | **3:44 PM** | 3:45 PM | Centurion Lounge |
| 8 | **6:04 PM** | 6:05 PM | Board Flight |

can you add this to a new path and deploy it? <!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>Walla Walla Winery Trip — May 1–2, 2026</title>
<style>
  @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playfair+Display:wght@400;600;700&family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600&display=swap');

  * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }

  body {
    font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
    background: #f8f5f0;
    color: #2d2926;
    line-height: 1.6;
  }

  .hero {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3b1f2b 0%, #5a2d3e 40%, #7a3e52 100%);
    color: white;
    padding: 60px 24px 48px;
    text-align: center;
  }

  .hero h1 {
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
    font-size: 2.6rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: 0.02em;
    margin-bottom: 8px;
  }

  .hero .subtitle {
    font-size: 1.1rem;
    font-weight: 300;
    opacity: 0.85;
    letter-spacing: 0.05em;
  }

  .container { max-width: 780px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 0 20px; }

  /* Day header */
  .day-header {
    margin-top: 48px;
    margin-bottom: 28px;
    padding-bottom: 12px;
    border-bottom: 2px solid #7a3e52;
  }

  .day-header h2 {
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
    font-size: 1.8rem;
    color: #3b1f2b;
  }

  .day-header .day-theme {
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    color: #7a3e52;
    font-weight: 500;
    margin-top: 4px;
    letter-spacing: 0.04em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
  }

  /* Stop card */
  .stop {
    background: white;
    border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 28px 28px 24px;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
    position: relative;
    border-left: 4px solid #7a3e52;
  }

  .stop-number {
    position: absolute;
    top: -12px;
    left: 20px;
    background: #7a3e52;
    color: white;
    width: 28px;
    height: 28px;
    border-radius: 50%;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    font-weight: 600;
  }

  .stop h3 {
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
    font-size: 1.35rem;
    color: #3b1f2b;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
  }

  .stop .time-badge {
    display: inline-block;
    background: #f3e8ed;
    color: #7a3e52;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    padding: 3px 12px;
    border-radius: 20px;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
  }

  .stop .meta {
    font-size: 0.88rem;
    color: #6b6560;
    margin-bottom: 14px;
  }

  .stop .meta span { display: block; margin-bottom: 3px; }

  .stop .vibe {
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    color: #4a4540;
    font-style: italic;
    margin-bottom: 10px;
  }

  .stop .details {
    font-size: 0.88rem;
    color: #5a5550;
  }

  .stop .details strong { color: #3b1f2b; }

  .tag {
    display: inline-block;
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    font-weight: 500;
    padding: 2px 10px;
    border-radius: 12px;
    margin-right: 6px;
    margin-top: 10px;
  }

  .tag.reservation { background: #fde8e8; color: #b91c1c; }
  .tag.walkin { background: #e8f5e8; color: #2d7a2d; }
  .tag.food { background: #fef3e2; color: #b45309; }

  /* Drive connector */
  .drive {
    text-align: center;
    padding: 8px 0;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    color: #9a918a;
    font-weight: 500;
    letter-spacing: 0.02em;
  }

  .drive::before { content: '↓  '; }
  .drive::after { content: '  ↓'; }

  /* Dinner card */
  .dinner {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, #3b1f2b, #5a2d3e);
    color: white;
    border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 28px;
    margin-bottom: 20px;
    margin-top: 8px;
  }

  .dinner h3 {
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
    font-size: 1.3rem;
    margin-bottom: 4px;
  }

  .dinner .time-badge {
    display: inline-block;
    background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
    color: white;
    font-weight: 600;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    padding: 3px 12px;
    border-radius: 20px;
    margin-bottom: 12px;
  }

  .dinner .meta { font-size: 0.88rem; opacity: 0.8; margin-bottom: 10px; }
  .dinner .meta span { display: block; margin-bottom: 3px; }
  .dinner .details { font-size: 0.9rem; opacity: 0.9; }

  /* Action items */
  .actions {
    background: white;
    border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 28px;
    margin: 40px 0 60px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
    border-left: 4px solid #b91c1c;
  }

  .actions h2 {
    font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
    font-size: 1.4rem;
    color: #b91c1c;
    margin-bottom: 16px;
  }

  .actions ol {
    padding-left: 20px;
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    color: #4a4540;
  }

  .actions li { margin-bottom: 10px; }
  .actions li strong { color: #3b1f2b; }

  .actions .note {
    margin-top: 16px;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
    color: #7a7570;
    font-style: italic;
    border-top: 1px solid #e8e4e0;
    padding-top: 12px;
  }

  /* Route summary */
  .route-summary {
    background: #f3e8ed;
    border-radius: 12px;
    padding: 20px 28px;
    margin-bottom: 28px;
    font-size: 0.88rem;
    color: #5a2d3e;
  }

  .route-summary strong { color: #3b1f2b; }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<div class="hero">
  <h1>Walla Walla Wine Weekend</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">May 1 – 2, 2026 &nbsp;·&nbsp; 6 wineries &nbsp;·&nbsp; 2 dinners</p>
</div>

<div class="container">

  <!-- ============ FRIDAY ============ -->
  <div class="day-header">
    <h2>Friday, May 1</h2>
    <div class="day-theme">Elevated + Scenic</div>
  </div>

  <div class="route-summary">
    <strong>Friday route:</strong> Caprio (south side) → Tranche (east side) → àMaurice (east side) → Downtown dinner. All stops within ~15 min of each other.
  </div>

  <!-- Stop 1 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">1</div>
    <h3>Caprio Cellars</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">11:00 AM reservation</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 1603 Whitely Rd, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-412-3054 &nbsp;·&nbsp; capriocellars.com</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">360° valley views · complimentary food + wine pairing · sets the tone for the whole trip</div>
    <div class="details">
      Bordeaux-inspired wines paired with scratch-made seasonal plates from Chef Williams. No tasting fee — the food pairing is included. This replaces lunch. Tastings limited to 20 people, so the room stays intimate. Plan for ~90 min.
    </div>
    <span class="tag reservation">Reservation required</span>
    <span class="tag food">Food pairing included</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~10 min drive via Mill Creek Rd</div>

  <!-- Stop 2 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">2</div>
    <h3>Tranche</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">1:00 PM reservation</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 705 Berney Dr, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-526-3500 &nbsp;·&nbsp; tranche.wine</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">Big views, structured reds · beautiful contrast after Caprio's intimacy</div>
    <div class="details">
      Known for estate-grown Bordeaux varieties and panoramic Blue Mountain views. May hours: Sun–Thu 11am–5pm, Fri–Sat 11am–9pm. Plan for ~60 min tasting.
    </div>
    <span class="tag reservation">Reservation recommended</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~12 min drive east toward Mill Creek</div>

  <!-- Stop 3 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">3</div>
    <h3>àMaurice Cellars</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">2:30 PM</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 178 Vineyard Ln, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-522-5444 &nbsp;·&nbsp; amaurice.com</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">Chill, pretty, slightly softer finish · ideal golden hour stop</div>
    <div class="details">
      Fri–Sat 10:30am–4:30pm. Relaxed, scenic setting with a softer wine profile than the earlier stops. Walk-ins welcome as space allows, but call ahead to be safe. Winds down by 4:30 — perfect timing to head to dinner. Plan ~60 min.
    </div>
    <span class="tag reservation">Reservation recommended</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~15 min drive to downtown</div>

  <!-- Friday dinner -->
  <div class="dinner">
    <h3>🍽️ Dinner — The Kitchen at Abeja</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">5:30 PM seating</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 2014 Mill Creek Rd, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-526-7400 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Book on Tock</span>
    </div>
    <div class="details">
      Five-course prix fixe ($135/person) with optional wine pairing ($55/person). Seven-course option at $165 + $65 pairing. Chef Mike Easton's monthly menu showcases Pacific Northwest ingredients paired with Abeja wines. Seatings Thu–Sun, 5:30–7:30pm. Book early — this is a destination dinner.
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- ============ SATURDAY ============ -->
  <div class="day-header">
    <h2>Saturday, May 2</h2>
    <div class="day-theme">Rocks District + Experience + Social</div>
  </div>

  <div class="route-summary">
    <strong>Saturday route:</strong> Rotie (Milton-Freewater, ~25 min south) → Abeja estate (east side, ~20 min back) → Downtown walk-around. Ends where you can park once and wander.
  </div>

  <!-- Stop 4 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">4</div>
    <h3>Rotie Cellars — Rocks Estate</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">11:00 AM reservation</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 84328 Trumbull Ln, Milton-Freewater, OR 97862</span>
      <span>📞 509-301-9074 &nbsp;·&nbsp; rotiecellars.com</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">Rocks District terroir · rugged, earthy, distinct</div>
    <div class="details">
      Appointment times: 11:00, 12:30, 2:00, 3:30. Open daily by appointment — call ahead for same-day. $40/person tasting fee, waived with $75 purchase. This is in The Rocks District (Milton-Freewater, OR) — unique cobblestone terroir you can feel in the wines. Plan ~75 min.
    </div>
    <span class="tag reservation">Reservation required</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~20 min drive north to Mill Creek Rd</div>

  <!-- Stop 5 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">5</div>
    <h3>Abeja Winery — Estate Tasting</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">1:00 PM reservation</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 2014 Mill Creek Rd, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-526-7400 &nbsp;·&nbsp; abeja.net</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">Your "moment" stop · walk the grounds, soak it in, slow down</div>
    <div class="details">
      The estate tasting room is reservation-only. Beautiful farmstead property with gardens and restored historic buildings. This is the experiential centerpiece of Saturday — don't rush it. Plan ~90 min including a walk around the grounds.
    </div>
    <span class="tag reservation">Reservation required</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~10 min drive to downtown</div>

  <!-- Stop 6 -->
  <div class="stop">
    <div class="stop-number">6</div>
    <h3>Downtown Walla Walla — Pick 1–2</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">3:00 – 5:30 PM flexible</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>All within a few blocks of each other downtown</span>
    </div>
    <div class="vibe">Social energy · vibe hop based on how you're feeling</div>
    <div class="details">
      <strong>DAMA Wines</strong> — 123 E Main St · Sat 12–6pm · fun, artsy, social<br>
      <strong>Kontos Cellars</strong> — 10 N 2nd Ave · Sat 11am–6:30pm · patio + easy drinking<br>
      <strong>Echolands Winery</strong> — 3281 Mill Creek Rd · Sat 11am–7pm · sleek + modern (note: estate location, not downtown)
    </div>
    <span class="tag walkin">Walk-in friendly</span>
  </div>

  <div class="drive">~5 min walk downtown</div>

  <!-- Saturday dinner -->
  <div class="dinner">
    <h3>🍽️ Dinner — Hattaway's on Alder</h3>
    <div class="time-badge">6:30 PM reservation</div>
    <div class="meta">
      <span>📍 125 W Alder St, Walla Walla, WA 99362</span>
      <span>📞 509-525-4433 &nbsp;·&nbsp; Book on Resy</span>
    </div>
    <div class="details">
      Southern-inspired fine dining with house-cured meats, local seafood, and a great local wine list. Sat hours 4–9pm. Intimate downtown spot — walkable from your last tasting. Pairs perfectly with a day of big reds: think smoked meats + rich Walla Walla Syrahs.
    </div>
  </div>

  <!-- ============ ACTION ITEMS ============ -->
  <div class="actions">
    <h2>Reservations To Book Now</h2>
    <ol>
      <li><strong>Caprio Cellars — Fri 11:00 AM</strong><br>Book via capriocellars.com or Tock. Non-negotiable, fills fast.</li>
      <li><strong>Tranche — Fri ~1:00 PM</strong><br>Call 509-526-3500 or book online. Recommended but not required.</li>
      <li><strong>àMaurice — Fri ~2:30 PM</strong><br>Call 509-522-5444 or email info@amaurice.com. Walk-ins OK but safer to call.</li>
      <li><strong>The Kitchen at Abeja — Fri 5:30 PM dinner</strong><br>Book on Tock (exploretock.com/the-kitchen-at-abeja). Book ASAP — limited seatings.</li>
      <li><strong>Rotie Cellars Rocks Estate — Sat 11:00 AM</strong><br>Call 509-301-9074. Appointment only.</li>
      <li><strong>Abeja Estate Tasting — Sat 1:00 PM</strong><br>Call 509-526-7400 or email wine@abeja.net. Reservation required.</li>
      <li><strong>Hattaway's on Alder — Sat 6:30 PM dinner</strong><br>Book on Resy (resy.com). Downtown, walkable from tastings.</li>
    </ol>
    <div class="note">
      Priority order: Caprio + The Kitchen at Abeja + Abeja estate tasting are the hardest to get. Book those first. Hattaway's and Rotie next. Tranche and àMaurice are more flexible.
    </div>
  </div>

</div>

</body>
</html>
